summary: Game randomly crashes in Singleplayer because of HashMap ConcurrentModificationException
description:
When attempting to play the game in singleplayer, there's a random chance the game might crash. Most of the time, it's HashMap#computeIfAbsent throwing ConcurrentModificationException.This issue happens because the hash map is the same object in both client and server in singleplayer. This creates a problem where the game can crash because when client thread runs computeIfAbsent on the map, server might add or remove entry from the map and vice versa.

This issue happens pretty sparsely and rarely because in the key is same in both threads most of the time which the exception isn’t thrown if an existing value gets changed when computeIfAbsent is running. This leaves pretty small time gap which makes it hard to reproduce. Though you can create a random string array, use them as keys and put them to HashMap, clear and redo it every tick in a method that runs in both threads to recreate it more easily.

As specified in JDK-8071667, this only happens if you use compute, computeIfAbsent, computeIfPresent or merge methods. It is specific to HashMap and other HashMap types.

In attachments there’s a method that recreates the crash which runs every player tick in both threads. strings is an array of 1000 random strings, map is String to String HashMap.
